channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Nearly 2.3 million passengers passed through Brussels Airport in May
item.description: Brussels Airport saw a 3.2% increase in passenger numbers despite a nationwide protest on 12 May and disruptions in the Middle East.

item.title: Fire breaks out at Place Jourdan brasserie in Brussels
item.description: Three staff members were treated at the scene by the emergency services, but did not require hospitalisation.

item.title: Brussels for kids: What to do with your family this weekend – 13 to 14 June
item.description: From film nights to indoor-outdoor comedy theatre, here are all the best activities to do with your family in Brussels this weekend.

item.title: The population of Brussels grew by only 39 people last year – here's why
item.description: According to newly released figures by Statbel, Belgium's statistics agency, the population of Brussels is stagnating.

item.title: 'Part of our DNA': Brussels protects city's nightlife amid series of nightclub closures
item.description: "The principle is simple: whoever causes the change takes responsibility. If you move in next to an existing club, you must take measures against noise nuisance."

item.title: Road closures and tram disruptions: What to expect during this summer's construction work
item.description: Brussels residents have been warned to expect disruptions this summer as the city ramps up its construction projects.

item.title: Brussels' Palais de Justice slowly breaking free of scaffolding
item.description: "An entire generation of Belgians has never really known the Palais de Justice without scaffolding. People aged 40 or over have seen it hidden behind a metal cage for almost their entire lives."

item.title: USG must pay €3,500 fine for pyrotechnics during Belgian cup final
item.description: In the 18th, 68th and 96th minutes, USG fans set off their fireworks, totalling at least 27 items.

item.title: Over €8 million seized in major money-laundering investigation in Brussels
item.description: The money is believed to have originated from drug trafficking in France, before being brought to Belgium by money couriers.

item.title: Three men accused of torture resulting in death in Brussels
item.description: The victim died on 11 August 2023 in the emergency department of the CHU Saint-Pierre hospital in Brussels, following several attempts to resuscitate him.

item.title: 'Bootcamp for rioters'? Defence Minister wants army training after Brussels protests
item.description: While Theo Francken has condemned student protesters for their behaviour last week, the police have also come under scrutiny for allegedly heavy-handed tactics.

item.title: Brussels Airport passengers face four-hour queues due to EU border control rules
item.description: This is not the first time non-EU passengers have faced hours-long queues at passport control in Brussels Airport.

item.title: The most and least expensive areas to buy an apartment in Brussels
item.description: In Brussels, apartment prices can vary sharply from one municipality to another.

item.title: Saint-Josse social housing agency accused of favouring friends and family
item.description: The agency managed around 230 private properties in the municipality until its closure at the end of last year due to bankruptcy.

item.title: Fare dodgers beware: STIB clampdown in Brussels leads to €7m collected in fines
item.description: More passengers on Brussels public transport were subjected to a ticket inspection than the city's official population in 2025.

item.title: No more 'institutional lasagna' as Brussels starts updating its administration
item.description: The Brussels Government has approved the "first and most important" part of the upcoming reform, aiming to cut expenses.

item.title: Saint-Gilles urged to 'permanently pedestrianise' school street near busy Barrière
item.description: An open letter to Saint-Gilles mayor Jean Spinette (PS) is asking for the creation of a "garden street" in front of the school.

item.title: 'Saviours of the Aegidium': Forgotten Brussels palace rescued by Georgian dissident
item.description: The large palace-like hall was a symbol of Brussels' roaring twenties, but has been left abandoned for over 40 years. That soon may change.

item.title: Two Brussels rivers set to see the light of day after decades underground
item.description: For much of the 20th century, many of Brussels' streams and rivers were channelled underground to make way for urban development.

item.title: Brussels wildlife rescue centre plans to build €6 million animal hospital
